title: Pan-Seared Tofu with Japanese Stir-Fry Sauce
description: A French-inspired dish featuring crispy tofu and a sweet, spicy stir-fry sauce. This recipe is perfect for a quick weeknight dinner.

ingredients.name: spicy
ingredients.notes: use a French hot sauce like harissa

ingredients.name: juicy
ingredients.notes: use a mixture of bell peppers and onions

ingredients.name: japanese
ingredients.notes: use soy sauce and sake or mirin

ingredients.name: fry
ingredients.notes: use a neutral oil like canola or grapeseed

ingredients.name: stir
ingredients.notes: use a small amount of cornstarch to thicken

instructions: Heat the oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the tofu and cook until golden brown on all sides, about 3-4 minutes.
Remove the tofu from the skillet and set aside.
Add the sliced bell peppers and onions to the skillet and cook until tender, about 3-4 minutes.
In a small bowl, whisk together the soy sauce, sake or mirin, and honey.
Pour the sauce over the vegetables and stir to combine.
Return the tofu to the skillet and toss to coat with the sauce.
Cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until the tofu is coated and the sauce is bubbly.
Serve hot, garnished with sesame seeds and chopped green onions if desired.
